coaching.title: Today's Run
coaching.message: Easy 6–8 miles, Z2 only (135–145 bpm), no vert work. You're in week 22 taper and your recovery score is 48/100—moderate but not alarming. Yesterday's 12-miler at 141 avg was solid, but you need to let that stimulus settle. Thursday is a traditional easy day anyway. Stay below 140 bpm; if you drift above it, dial it back. This is about maintaining aerobic feel without adding stress to the nervous system with 85 days to the race.

coaching.title: Fuel Right
coaching.message: You're averaging 4.77 hours sleep over 30 days—that's low for an athlete in taper. Priority today: carb-load slightly above baseline (aim for 6–8g per kg bodyweight) and front-load breakfast and lunch before your run. During the easy 6–8 miler, you don't need gels unless you're extending beyond 90 minutes. Post-run, get 20–30g protein and 60–80g carbs within 30 minutes to support recovery and glycogen repletion before Friday's shakeout.

coaching.title: Recovery
coaching.message: Your sleep efficiency is 73% (acceptable) but your absolute volume is the issue—6 hours last night, 4.77-hour average. With travel for work, I know you're constrained, but aim for 7+ hours tonight and Friday. No caffeine after 2 p.m. The Julian Alps requires sharp legs and a clear head; sleep debt will erode both. If you're traveling, negotiate one night in a quiet hotel without meetings before 8 a.m.

coaching.title: Mental Edge
coaching.message: You're 85 days out and in the final taper phase. Mentally, this is where doubts creep in—the volume is dropping, intensity is selective, and your brain wants to prove fitness. Resist it. Trust the 22 weeks of work. Your last 12-miler with 3,832 ft vert is proof the engine is built. Use these next two weeks to visualize the alpine terrain, walk through aid station logistics, and get your head calm. Race day execution beats a perfect training log.
